# UC-22.62.5 · CERT-In Direction (v) VPN/VPS/cloud-provider subscriber-KYC + 5-year retention with Aadhaar/PAN validation + IP-allocation chain-of-custody

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Audit &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Indian VPN and cloud providers must keep detailed customer records — including identity, address, and how long they used the service — for 5 years after the customer leaves. We check daily that every customer record is complete and that we still have the records of customers who left up to 5 years ago.*

---

## Description

Enumerates every VPN/VPS/cloud subscriber against the seven mandatory KYC limbs of CERT-In Direction (v) and risk-scores on completeness and strength of validation: (i) validated name, (ii) period of hire, (iii) IPs allotted, (iv) email + IP + timestamp at registration, (v) purpose, (vi) validated address, (vii) ownership pattern. Validation strength is rated against Aadhaar/PAN/video-KYC. A separate limb tracks 5-year post-deregistration retention.

## Value

Direction (v) is the limb of the CERT-In Directions most contentious for international VPN providers — several (NordVPN, ExpressVPN, Surfshark, Proton, etc.) withdrew Indian-region servers rather than comply. For Indian VPN/VPS/cloud providers, the practical risk is twofold: (i) regulatory action for non-compliance, and (ii) inability to respond to a Section 70B(6) CERT-In information request (the data simply isn't there). The 5-year post-deregistration retention is the most commonly under-implemented limb.

## Implementation

(1) Wire the CRM / subscriber-management system to `hec:vpn:vps:cloud:subscriber:kyc` on every onboarding, change, and deregistration. (2) Enforce the seven KYC limbs in the onboarding form (server-side validation). (3) Implement a 5-year post-deregistration retention policy on the subscriber-management database (legal-hold flag preventing physical deletion). (4) Schedule UC-22.62.5 daily; route `risk_score = 100` to the COO + CISO at severity P1.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.62.5` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `cert_in_clause="CERT-In-Dir-5-1"`. Every subscriber pins (subscriber_id, kyc_status, kyc_validation_method, hire_start_at, hire_end_at, ips_allotted, retention_due_until, retention_compliant, risk_score).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Onboard a new VPS subscriber with name, address, email, and IP allocation but DO NOT validate Aadhaar/PAN and DO NOT capture ownership-pattern. UC-22.62.5 MUST flag with risk_score >= 80.

### Negative scenario

Onboard the same subscriber with all 7 KYC limbs completed (validated name, validated Aadhaar/PAN-anchored address, validated contact, validated email + IP + timestamp at registration, recorded purpose, recorded ownership pattern, hire-start date, IPs allotted). UC-22.62.5 MUST record risk_score=0.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- A CRM / subscriber-management system with API hooks for onboarding events.
- UIDAI Aadhaar e-KYC API access via a registered KYC User Agency (KUA) or an Indian-regulated KYC service-provider.
- Indian-jurisdiction subscriber database (UC-22.62.4 retention compliant).

## Step 1 - Subscriber KYC KV Store

```ini
[cert_in_subscriber_kyc_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.subscriber_id = string
field.validated_name = string
field.validated_address = string
field.kyc_validation_method = string
field.registration_email = string
field.registration_ip = string
field.registration_timestamp = time
field.purpose_of_hire = string
field.ownership_pattern = string
field.hire_start_at = time
field.hire_end_at = time
field.deregistered_at = time
field.b2b_entity_kyc_ref = string
field.non_aadhaar_validation_method = string
field.legacy_pre_2022 = bool
```

## Step 2 - IP-allocation register

```ini
[cert_in_ip_allocation_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.subscriber_id = string
field.ip_address = string
field.allocated_at = time
field.released_at = time
```

## Step 3 - Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.62.5: CERT-In Subscriber KYC]
cron_schedule = 0 5 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -1d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.62.5,reg=CERT-In,clause=CERT-In-Dir-5-1
alert.severity = 4
```

## Step 4 - Indian-jurisdiction-specific notes

- **No-log VPN claims**: a 'no-log' VPN marketing claim is incompatible with Direction (v); providers must choose between Indian-region operations and the no-log brand.
- **Reseller chain**: where a reseller is the immediate counterparty, the principal must require flow-through KYC from the reseller — load `reseller_kyc_chain_ref`.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup cert_in_subscriber_kyc_lookup
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval kyc_name_present = if(isnotnull(validated_name) AND validated_name != "", 1, 0)
| eval kyc_period_present = if(isnotnull(hire_start_at), 1, 0)
| eval kyc_ips_present = if(isnotnull(ips_allotted) AND ips_allotted != "", 1, 0)
| eval kyc_email_ts_present = if(isnotnull(registration_email) AND isnotnull(registration_ip) AND isnotnull(registration_timestamp), 1, 0)
| eval kyc_purpose_present = if(isnotnull(purpose_of_hire), 1, 0)
| eval kyc_address_present = if(isnotnull(validated_address), 1, 0)
| eval kyc_ownership_present = if(isnotnull(ownership_pattern), 1, 0)
| eval kyc_limbs_complete = kyc_name_present + kyc_period_present + kyc_ips_present + kyc_email_ts_present + kyc_purpose_present + kyc_address_present + kyc_ownership_present
| eval kyc_validation_method_strong = if(kyc_validation_method IN ("aadhaar-otp", "aadhaar-biometric", "pan-validated", "in-person-kyc", "video-kyc"), 1, 0)
| eval days_since_deregistration = if(isnotnull(deregistered_at), round((now_ts - deregistered_at) / 86400, 1), null())
| eval retention_due_until_ts = if(isnotnull(deregistered_at), deregistered_at + (5 * 365.25 * 86400), null())
| eval retention_compliant = case(
    isnull(deregistered_at), 1,
    isnotnull(retention_due_until_ts) AND now_ts <= retention_due_until_ts, 1,
    1=1, 0)
| eval retention_breach = if(isnotnull(deregistered_at) AND days_since_deregistration < 1825 AND retention_compliant = 0, 1, 0)
| eval risk_score = case(
    kyc_limbs_complete < 5, 100,
    kyc_limbs_complete = 5 OR kyc_limbs_complete = 6, 80,
    kyc_validation_method_strong = 0 AND kyc_limbs_complete = 7, 60,
    retention_breach = 1, 95,
    1=1, 0)
| where risk_score > 0
| sort 0 -risk_score, +kyc_limbs_complete
| table subscriber_id kyc_limbs_complete kyc_validation_method kyc_validation_method_strong hire_start_at deregistered_at retention_due_until_ts retention_compliant risk_score
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) KYC-limb-coverage waterfall. (Panel 2) Validation-method mix. (Panel 3) 5-year retention coverage. (Panel 4) New-subscriber onboarding-quality trend.

## Known False Positives

1. **B2B enterprise subscriber**: for an enterprise body-corporate subscriber, the KYC limbs apply to the body-corporate (CIN, GST, registered address, beneficial owners) rather than to individual employees — load `b2b_entity_kyc_ref` and apply the body-corporate KYC schema. 2. **Aadhaar prohibition**: some sectors / customer-types may not lawfully use Aadhaar for KYC (e.g., DBT-only) — load `non_aadhaar_validation_method` and treat as strong if PAN/passport/video-KYC. 3. **Pre-2022 vintage subscribers**: subscribers onboarded before 27 June 2022 may have incomplete legacy KYC — load `legacy_pre_2022 = true` and treat as `kyc_limbs_complete = 7` after a one-time top-up KYC.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `cert_in_22_62_5_exceptions` with the COO and CISO documented approval, an explicit subscriber_id scope, an expiry date, and a written justification.

## References

- [CERT-In Directions of 28 April 2022 — Direction (v) on VPN/VPS/cloud KYC](https://www.cert-in.org.in/PDF/CERT-In_Directions_70B_28.04.2022.pdf)
- [CERT-In FAQ on Direction (v)](https://www.cert-in.org.in/PDF/FAQs.pdf)
- [UIDAI Aadhaar e-KYC API specification](https://uidai.gov.in/)
